reviews.review_text: The game is a legend a foundation and skeleton for the dying light games. It's not as good as dying light but the zombies are still very creepy even on the final stages of the game. Overall i'll rate this game 8.5/10
In alot of ways, this game is a mess. But the combat is quite fun, which is the most important thing. I love the stamina system

Legendary game back on the 360.
The definitive edition is actually worse than the og though. This really started the trend of "definitive" just meaning brighter and overblown with post FX.
Mods can disable those though, but the worst offender is the head-bob. Game is unplayable for many people because of the impossible to disable head bob. Its hurting my eyes so much I cant take it anymore. This game probably contributed to ruining some of my days in childhood because of it.

I used to like this game but it just has too many annoyances that hurt it and ruin the fun

First of all is the movement, moving around is so slow and sticky like it just constantly feel like you're getting stuck to everything when in narrow areas and this really doesn't help when you're trying to fight 20 zombies, changing direction, activating sprint and jumping has like half a second of input delay.

Second is weapon durability, EVERY single melee weapon seems to be made of balsa wood and breaks down way too fast, you'd think that would be excusable for sticks, wood planks, boat paddles but weapons that should be tough and sturdy such as hammers, baseball bats, crowbars, wrenches, machetes even when fully upgraded and getting the skill perks break after killing like 10-20 zombies, yes ZOMBIES! made of rotting flesh and bones not concrete and reinforced titanium. Atleast unlike in Dead Island 2 the weapons actually feel like they do damage, it doesn't take 30 hits to kill a zombie here but it still isn't worth it since every weapon takes as much damage as it deals.

Third is trading, please explain why the f**k these guys are charging hundreds of dollars for a random piece of junk that you can find lying around anywhere? one guy charged $600 for 1 magnet but if I sell him a magnet he'll only give me $8 and even worse they'll charge thousand for a low level basic weapon but if I sell them a fully upgraded modified high level weapon they only give me $100, that just seems broken, while I'm on the subject lets talk about the molotov traders, so I can give 5 bottles of whiskey to this guy and he'll make me some molotovs, sounds good right? because if I give him 5 whiskey bottles he's gonna give me 5 molotovs? NOPE he only gives me 1, how is that fair?

Then there's things like escort missions, I've always hated them in games because whoever you're protecting is always defenceless and oblivious to danger but this game just takes it to another level, whoever you're supposed to protect is an absolute moron and is just asking to die, especially the russian guy from the helicopter crash, I'm supposed to escort him to a nearby bunker, just ahead is a group of zombies, I try to play it smart and throw a molotov at the zombies but instead this "genius" decides to run directly into of the group of burning zombies and almost die.

Then there's act 2, act 1 is good, the gameplay seems fair but the moment you leave the resort and enter the city the gameplay just becomes total BS, the game is just non stop spamming the runners at you, there's no point of fighting them because 1 they constantly respawn, 2 all you're weapons will be broken within 2 minutes, 3 they can now kill you in 3 hits even after levelling up a bunch.

Played Dead Island 2 first. Going to this after was disappointing.
The prolog played like poo, the looting requires you to loot the same box multiple times, the weapon equip system is crap... I'm sure it's a decent game, but it left a bad first impression.

First off, this game looks amazing and runs great even on low specs, and is generally pretty well optimized with minimal frame drops or stuttering. Although some key differences to the original Dead Island (2011) are very noticeable to those of us who played the original: the lighting, the physics, the ragdoll & dismemberment animations, and most egregiously, the horrendous-looking Dying Light gun models. The lighting in the original was more gloomy and ominous and contrasted the tropical backdrop very well, and in this Definitive Edition, the saturation was turned all the way up and many areas appear way brighter than they should be. Many physics objects are now static and bolted to the ground for some reason, and in addition to this, the broken limbs on zombies no longer flail around and their ragdoll animations are less dynamic; the zombie models look arguably worse with less personality than those in the original as well. Dying Light is a great game, but why did they have to directly import a significant chunk of its assets and models instead of just updating the old ones from the original game? (Pretty gross and lazy from a development standpoint).

All of the above criticisms also apply to DI: Riptide DE as well, but to newcomers these nitpicks won't really matter - this is still a fun Open-World Zombie Action RPG at the end of the day - and this version also has some admittedly nice QoL improvements if you already own the originals and want to pick this up on sale sometime.
This game is fantastic, it's straight to the point, the graphics hold well, combat is visceral, it runs and plays well, I'm happy with this purchase.

I think it’s better to play this with a friend. I finished it solo, and it was a tedious and annoying journey. Not all of it was bad, though — it had some spooky moments, especially at the start of the game on the beach resort. However, things start to go downhill once you reach the city section.

The game didn’t age well and suffers from a lot of technical issues. I can’t recommend it unless you’re a fan of zombie games, since it’s considered a cult classic, or if you want to see how it inspired the creation of the Dying Light series.

A fantastic remaster of a classic that perfected the tropical zombie paradise!

Dead Island Definitive Edition is the absolute best way to experience the game that started it all. The graphical overhaul brings Banoi Island to life with beautiful lighting, upgraded textures, and a smooth framerate. Waking up in a luxury tropical resort turned into a bloodbath creates an unforgettable horror atmosphere.

The first-person melee combat feels heavy and brutal. Scavenging for blueprints and upgrading your weapons from regular machetes to electrified, limb-chopping tools of destruction is incredibly addictive. The dynamic between the distinct playable characters adds great replayability, especially when teaming up for 4-player co-op. It is a nostalgic, atmospheric, and highly entertaining zombie survival classic.

The character movement is terrible. Just trying to move left or right feels choppy and clunky instead of smooth. The graphics look awful and lack any artistic direction. Combat feels incredibly stiff—slaying zombies just isn't smooth or satisfying at all.

The game is also riddled with bugs, like audio cutting out and a glitched achievement system where achievements constantly pop up and disappear on repeat, yet show as unearned once you restart the game on Steam. On top of that, the UI looks terrible and the mouse sensitivity is poorly optimized. It honestly feels like a cheap, lazy PC port made just for a quick cash grab, completely missing the mark on what makes a game fun. Not worth the money, and definitely not worth the time

A fun, relatively glitchy mess. Great game to play with friends.
story: 5/10
gameplay: 7/10
enjoyability: 10/10

Sprint simply does not work. 

I was really excited to play this game since it's been ages since I did on the Xbox 360, but unfortunately the sprint interaction just doesn't work. I've tried every single "fix" I could find on the internet and nothing worked. My character simply starts the sprint animation, and then immediately cancels sprint. 

It's just kind of crazy to me how the "Definitive Edition" of a game has such a blatant issue that just never gets addressed.

worth buying but its very repetitive. having friends for this is recommended

PLAY THE CONSOLE VERSION, yet another game that has a very bad pc port. The gameplay is really fun and the story is simple but pretty alright with a bunch of pretty cool or pretty annoying side quests throughout. There is a ton of backtracking and back and fourth between areas due to these side missions opening up and just padding the time just a bit, same with the follow and protect quests. I enjoy the unique zombies including the rams and butchers, which are pretty memorable.
All in all, has some issues and a little basic but you will have fun taking out large amounts of zombies all over the island of Banoi and hopefully get something out of the story 
7.5/10
Ryder White's Campaign is a really great short straight-forward dlc(1.5 to 2.5 hours) that follows the other side of the story that you truly don't fully understand unless you play this and I honestly wish it was apart of the main game just because I know many people likely never even played this. 
7.0/10
will come back for a playthrough every few years, one of my favorite games and the og was the game that made me get steam. still unmatched zombie slaughtering and atmosphere

This game start off pretty mediocre, and turn good only about 25% into it.
Also, this game is on sale pretty often, so I'd suggest getting it then.
And, Dying Light is still much better.
Had this game for the xbox 360 and played countless hours. It's nice to play it again

Most missions are really just fetch quests and a just a copy paste of: Go to [PLACE], clear the zombies and get [ITEM]. But that is honestly its only weakness. This game is fun. The combat is fun, the world is cool and the characters are funny too.

Now don't get this misunderstood. This is not a super story driven game, with an amazing narrative or crazy visuals. This is a good hack and slash, with fun mechanics and funny characters.
If you're looking for a zombie game that doesn't take itself too seriously and to have some fun slaying zombies then this is the game for you. But if you expect anything more, then keep looking.

Had some fun with this. Had to check some strategies as I was dying a lot early on but yeeting axes into zombies faces is rarely boring. 

Only major gripe was zombies that throw their weapons is a very annoying one hit kill.

Played smoothly on Steam Deck too. Doesn't put stay itself welcome either.

The first couple hours are greate due to the innovating scenery and engagin issues of the first act, but with the stupid "Go here -> Kill/Fetch this -> Get back" it quickly turns from a zombies videogame into a zombies chore, around the time you open the bunkers you are just waiting for the gma eto finish. Progression is non existant, you level up for meaningless perks, your weapns don't last and the zombies permanently have a slight edge on you to make sure you just don't cheese yuour way through the game; but great news, cheesing the game is the only way to make it bearable, abure the stupid IA to run straight from side to side of the map, use sharp guns as ninja stars and hope you don't get a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ dead because of the horrible hitboxes and absurd damage you take.

Somehow this game feels like the earliest version of those half-baked parmanently early-access we'll-fix-it-with-a-DLC kind of game; nothing in this game feels finshed nor polished, it seems that ran out of money half way chapter one, and the only reedeming quality it has is it's amazing graphics for the year it launched

Failed experiment leads to a horrific tragedy for the entire island. First, the main character selection process involves each player with their own background and skills. The starting point is a coastal area with survivors. Some of the missions are quite exciting, including side quests. Numerous secret items are scattered around, and random NPCs will give new missions with varying rewards. The zombies here will grow stronger as your character levels up. Upgrades to melee weapons and firearms are characterized by different colors. Each act will move to a new area, and the map will be surprisingly expansive.

[h2]tl;dr[/h2] 

with gore as big USP, it is fun for a playthrough but ultimately kinda forgettable 

[h2]Overview[/h2]

Welcome back to Banoi. The Dead Island Definitive Edition drops you into the aftermath of a catastrophic zombie outbreak on a tropical island that was supposed to be a paradise. You step into the shoes of one of four distinct survivors, each with their own backstory and fighting style, tasked with navigating the chaos, uncovering the truth behind the infection, and trying to escape alive. Whether you choose to tackle the nightmare solo or team up with friends in co-op, the core loop remains the same: explore the sprawling environment, scavenge for weapons, and brutally dismember anything that moves.

The package includes the original campaign, the "Bloodbath Arena", and the "Ryder White" DLC, effectively offering the complete experience from the original release.

[h2]The Good Stuff[/h2]

First and foremost: goregoregore. If you are here for the visceral satisfaction of tearing limbs off, this game still delivers. The physics are chaotic, and watching a zombie get dismembered never really gets old. What shall I say, I am a simple man. 

https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3728331611
Exploration is decent and Banoi feels alive. There's a certain joy in just wandering around. Especially the initial resort area may be the most appealing environment in the franchise. The game is not really that hard, but you have to be careful. Get brazen, and even minor zombies will stomp you. It is a nice mix, not too hard, but not a pure walk in the park either. The playable characters feel distinct enough, and the tone is rather serious, which fits the setting.

https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3714362011
It may be a weird thing to praise, but the Dead Island Save Editor exists. Using it can lead to outcomes that are quite hilarious and simply fun. While I actually encourage fooling around with it, you should genuinely finish the game at least once to not ruin the experience. On the other hand ... do whatever the f you want, I'm not your mother.

[h2]The Sorta-Kinda-Grinds-My-Gears-Stuff[/h2]

The story? Like whatever. It's run-of-the-mill which is not necessarily terrible, but really nothing special. The writing leaves much to be desired. Sidequests are absurdly redundant and mostly irrelevant fetch quests that can wear you down after some time. 

From a technical standpoint, it is still a bit rough around the edges. You can get stuck, and turning your head while driving a car has an immense delay that makes driving kinda awkward. In some scenarios you may need to jump because a set of stairs or a curb won't let you just continue walking. It feels clumsy and may lead to enemy related flusterclucks. The "one punch mode" you unlock after finishing the story is fun at first but ultimately not really worth your time.

The Ryder White DLC was off-putting at first, but it turns out to be a huge love letter to Left 4 Dead as I came to realize that it plays just like a good ol' L4D campaign. Taking this into consideration it is kinda nice. It is also quite the change to have a very FPS heavy segment, as the main game is rather focused on melee combat. BUT ... 

[h2]The Atrociously-Bad Stuff[/h2]

... everything FPS in this game is absolutely terrible. Seriously. It doesn't feel engaging or rewarding. And the Bloodbath Arena DLC is simply a sorry excuse for content. It's a Killing Floor mode that is complete and utter trash and zero fun. Either design a game around the concept or just leave it. No DLC would have been better than that. But this is a huge me-thing. I despise the horde concept if it is supposed to make you feel like a valuable addition handed to you by the benign devs. Those resources should be focused on the main story experience.

Furthermore, many of my gripes with the game come down to achievement hunting ...

[h2]Achievement-Hunter-Things[/h2]

... as the achievement-design is unfortunately pretty bad. It is reliant on over-the-top co-op achievements. Having to play with 10 different players for at least 15 minutes or playing with a full team for example feels just odd. I love my SP experiences and forcing co-op this hard on you for a game that can be enjoyed solo should be forbidden. Considering the age of the game, gathering enough players can be quite the challenge. It's not worth my time, but people looking to deal with that are active in the community hub if you want to take your chances. 

There are surprisingly few story or exploration-related achievements. Many are for specific kills, some are grindy and repetitive. Finishing Act 1 with each character simply comes down to mundane busywork. If you are looking to be efficient, it may be prudent to pick Sam B, as there is an achievement ("Oh, no you don't") that requires a specific ability only he possesses. As much as it troubles a lifelong Xian Mei simp like me to say that, it ultimately may save some time. 

For the sake of completeness, certain challenges may be banalized with save edits. Get creative. 

[h2]Conclusion[/h2]

Dead Island really fosters the idea of replayability, but offers nothing to make it worthwhile. Hence, the things that annoy me mostly relate to full completion and replays. If you don't care for all that, you will be offered a one-time experience which is pretty decent. Slaughtering zombies will forever be fun. But it also won't be something that you will vividly remember for the rest of your life. It is very cheap in sales, so there's really nothing you can do wrong.

Replaying this game in 2025 really shows its age. It's still fun, but it feels like a completely different game from when I first played it years ago. Maybe it's because I was much younger, or maybe it's because I played it with three other people. I'm not entirely sure.

The achievement spread is very annoying. Having to play all four characters up to a certain point is pretty lame because the game doesn't change enough between playthroughs. The unique powers, character shadows, and voice lines aren't enough to make me want to replay the first act four separate times.

The jungle still sucks, both visually and from a gameplay perspective.

I do suggest you play the game [b]once[/b].
